title: Sukh Sroay on X: "A LATVIAN GUY WITH A €3.5M BUDGET JUST BEAT PIXAR AT THE OSCARS. Disney had $200 million. He had Blender. A free app. His film is called Flow. It won Best Animated Feature at the 97th Academy Awards. Beat Inside Out 2. Beat The Wild Robot. Beat every studio with a thousand https://t.co/tv0yUItG8t" / X

tweets.author:
Sukh Sroay
@sukh_saroy
tweets.text:
A LATVIAN GUY WITH A €3.5M BUDGET JUST BEAT PIXAR AT THE OSCARS.

Disney had $200 million. He had Blender. A free app.

His film is called Flow. It won Best Animated Feature at the 97th Academy Awards. Beat Inside Out 2. Beat The Wild Robot. Beat every studio with a thousand employees and a rendering farm.

The whole thing was made in Blender.

Free. Open source. GPL-3.0. Zero subscription. No Adobe. No Autodesk. No Maya. No $10,000/seat Houdini license.

Just a 30-year-old director from Latvia, a small team, and a tool you can download right now for free.

Here's the wildest part:

Adobe Creative Cloud Pro costs $69.99/month. $839.88/year. Autodesk Maya costs $235/month. $2,820/year.

Blender costs $0. It costs $0 per month. $0 per year. $0 forever.

And a movie made in it just walked into Hollywood and took the top prize.

→ Full 3D modeling, rigging, and animation
→ Compositing, motion tracking, and VFX
→ Video editing built in
→ Sculpting tools
→ Simulation: fire, smoke, fluid, cloth, particles
→ Cycles and EEVEE render engines
→ Python scripting API
→ Runs on Windows, Mac, and Linux

Netflix joined the Blender Development Fund. Epic Games funds it. Pixar uses it internally. And a Latvian indie film just proved it can win an Oscar.

One honest note: Blender has a steep learning curve. The first week is confusing. But every tutorial you need is free on YouTube and it has 25+ full-time developers paid by donations.

Your creative software costs thousands a year. The software that just beat Pixar costs nothing.

Repo in the first comment.
